transcript:
Agent: City Power and Light, emergency line. This is Carlos. What's the nature of your emergency?
Customer: Hi. My whole neighborhood has been without power since midnight. It's now 8 AM and we still have nothing. I have medical equipment that needs power.
Agent: I understand — that's a serious concern. Let me check the outage map for your area. Can I get your address?
Customer: 4821 Maple Drive, zip code 60614.
Agent: Thank you. I can confirm there is an active outage affecting 340 customers in that grid sector. Crews were dispatched at 3 AM. The estimated restoration time is 10 AM today.
Customer: Two more hours? I have a CPAP machine and my husband needs it to breathe properly at night. We've been struggling all night.
Agent: I completely understand the urgency. Because you have medical equipment dependency, I'm flagging your account as a medical priority. This means your address will be prioritized once crews reach that section of the grid.
Customer: Is there anything else you can do?
Agent: I'm also registering you in our Medical Baseline Program which provides additional protections and advance outage notifications. I'd also like to offer to connect you with our emergency equipment assistance line — they can help arrange a temporary generator for medical equipment cases.
Customer: That would be very helpful.
Agent: I'm transferring the request now. You should receive a call within 20 minutes. The reference number for your case is OUT-20240216-4821.
Customer: Thank you so much. I was getting really worried.
Agent: Completely understandable. We take medical needs very seriously. Is there anything else I can help with right now?
Customer: No, just please get the power back as soon as possible.
Agent: Crews are working on it. You'll receive a text update when power is restored. Thank you for calling and stay safe.
